channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sandeep Pathak Reacts to Two FIRs Filed Against Him, Denies Any Knowledge of the Cases
item.description: Sandeep Pathak, the BJP leader and Rajya Sabha MP, addressed the media after reports surfaced about two FIRs being lodged against him. Speaking in Delhi, Pathak clarified that he had not been informed...

item.title: How Canadian travellers can reduce luggage fees with practical packing tips
item.description: Air Canada and WestJet have recently increased their fees for checked luggage, adding $10 to the first and second checked bags and $50 to excess, overweight, and oversized bags. Rising airline costs, ...

item.title: The Notwithstanding Clause: A Historical Constitutional Bargain that Shouldn't be Altered by Courts
item.description: The article addresses the controversial role of Section 33, the notwithstanding clause, in the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms. On the 44th anniversary of the Charter’s adoption, Justice Minis...

item.title: Treasurer flags possible housing tax reforms to improve intergenerational fairness
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has indicated that the upcoming federal budget may include measures to address 'intergenerational fairness', particularly in the housing market. Chalmers highlighted concerns ab...

item.title: Vosloorus spaza shop owner returns safely after going missing
item.description: Mazwi Kubheka, a 27-year-old spaza shop owner from Marimba Gardens in Vosloorus, who was reported missing a month ago, has been found safe. Gauteng police spokesperson, Col Dimakatso Nevhuhulwi, confi...

item.title: Tanzanian national Anthony Juma detained in Kenya for suspected murder of 11-year-old Glorious Maore
item.description: A Tanzanian citizen named Anthony Juma, known as Kipara, is currently in police custody in Kenya. He is the main suspect in the brutal killing of 11-year-old Glorious Maore. According to Kenyan Direct...

item.title: Analysis of the Liberal Government’s Fiscal Policy and Deficit Trends
item.description: Over more than a decade in power, the Liberal Party of Canada, led initially by Justin Trudeau and now by Mark Carney, has repeatedly altered or abandoned its fiscal rules, resulting in persistent bud...

item.title: Survivors Recall Tragic Jabalpur Boat Accident
item.description: The horrific Jabalpur boat tragedy, which occurred recently in Madhya Pradesh, has left the survivors with traumatic memories. According to the survivors, the incident unfolded suddenly and tragically...

item.title: Iran Proposes 30-Day Deadline for U.S. to End Conflict
item.description: Iran has proposed a new 14-point plan aimed at bringing an end to the ongoing war with the U.S. within 30 days, according to state media reports. The proposal, which contradicts a previous U.S. plan, ...

item.title: Two Men Killed in Separate Incidents in Bajaur, Including Social Activist
item.description: In Bajaur district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, two separate violent incidents on Sunday led to the deaths of two men, including a prominent social activist. Malik Ziaur Rehman, aged 37, was shot by unident...

item.title: Weekly roundup of global nuclear issues, policy debates, and geopolitical tensions
item.description: This weekly newsletter compiles a wide range of developments related to nuclear energy, weapons, environmental risks, and global politics. It blends news coverage, opinion pieces, and curated links, o...

item.title: Challenges and Unrealistic Expectations in Canadian Politics and Infrastructure
item.description: The article by Chris Selley critiques what he perceives as unrealistic political and infrastructural expectations in Canada. Selley warns that after external pressures, such as Donald Trump's policies...

item.title: Dar Youth League kicks off with strong participation and big wins in opening matches
item.description: The 2026 Dar Youth League has officially kicked off in Dar es Salaam, bringing together more than 170 young footballers from eight teams in a growing grassroots competition. The teams taking part incl...

item.title: President Marcos directs prompt action amid Mayon Volcano ashfall
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has directed government agencies to take immediate and coordinated measures in response to the recent ashfall from Mayon Volcano. The volcanic activity has affected surr...

item.title: Nigerian Army Confiscates Stolen Crude Oil and Diesel, Arrests Nine Suspects in Niger Delta
item.description: The Nigerian Army don announce say dem don destroy 10 illegal oil refining sites and arrest nine people wey dem suspect dey involved for oil theft for Niger Delta. During operations wey last almost on...

item.title: Louis Vuitton Australia raises dividend to parent company amid 2025 profit growth
item.description: Louis Vuitton’s Australian operations have raised their dividend payment to its parent company, which is owned by billionaire Bernard Arnault, following a profitable 2025. The company’s net profit ros...

item.title: Victorian Liberals win Nepean byelection but face challenges ahead of state election
item.description: In the Nepean byelection, the Victorian Liberal Party, led by Jess Wilson, achieved a win by fending off challenges from One Nation and a community independent. The party increased its two-candidate-p...

item.title: Gov Dauda Lawal stresses strong political base for effective governance during APC Zamfara Congress
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State don talk say the strength of any government dey depend on how strong im political base be. Him talk this one during the All Progressives Congress (APC) Zamfara St...

item.title: Addressing the Overlap Between Substance Abuse and Mental Health in South Africa
item.description: Substance abuse in South Africa is intricately linked to untreated mental health conditions, creating a crisis that requires urgent government intervention. Mental health challenges have been rising g...

item.title: Laborda's Late Goal Earns Whitecaps 1-1 Draw Against Galaxy
item.description: In a tightly contested match between the Vancouver Whitecaps and LA Galaxy, Mathias Laborda scored an equalizer in the 82nd minute to seal a 1-1 draw on Saturday night. The Whitecaps, who had been on ...
